---
title: "exercise_4"
format: html
editor: visual
---

# Exercise 4

Using the optimal number of cores in your case, compare (benchmark) the performance of the method you used to solve the previous exercise and the same using mirai and in_parallel instead. For this, modify the script in the previous exercise to add this comparison at the end.

```{r}
# Benchmark with mirai and do_parallel
# libraries
library(mirai)
library(parallel)
library(foreach)
library(doParallel)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(bench)

# Optimal number of cores according to previous benchmark
optimal_cores <- 8

# Method 1: parallel::parLapply with optimal number of cores

run_parLapply_optimal <- function() {
  
  cl <- parallel::makeCluster(optimal_cores)
  on.exit(parallel::stopCluster(cl), add = TRUE)
  
  parallel::clusterExport(
    cl,
    varlist = c("iris_data", "coef_function", "n_repetitions"),
    envir = environment()
  )
  
  parallel::clusterSetRNGStream(cl, iseed = 123)
  
  parallel::parLapply(
    cl,
    X = seq_len(n_repetitions),
    fun = coef_function
  )
}

# Method 2: purrr::map with mirai::in_parallel

run_mirai_optimal <- function() {
  
  mirai::daemons(optimal_cores)
  on.exit(mirai::daemons(0), add = TRUE)
  
  purrr::map(
    seq_len(n_repetitions),
    purrr::in_parallel(
      \(repetition) {
        
        sample_individuals <- sample(
          seq_len(nrow(iris_data)),
          nrow(iris_data),
          replace = TRUE
        )
        
        boot_data <- iris_data[sample_individuals, ]
        
        model_res <- suppressWarnings(
          stats::glm(
            Species ~ Petal.Length,
            data = boot_data,
            family = stats::binomial()
          )
        )
        
        stats::coefficients(model_res)
      },
      iris_data = iris_data
    )
  )
}

# Benchmark comparison

parallel_comparison_benchmark <- bench::mark(
  parLapply_optimal = run_parLapply_optimal(),
  mirai_in_parallel = run_mirai_optimal(),
  iterations = 3,
  check = FALSE,
  memory = FALSE,
  time_unit = "s"
)

parallel_comparison_benchmark <- data.frame(parallel_comparison_benchmark)

```

-   Is there any differences? Answer in a comment in the script. Yes, results from the benchmark show a improvement in the total time of 5 seconds using mirai over doParallel with 8 cores.

Yes, results from the benchmark show a improvement in the total time of 5 seconds using mirai over doParallel with 8 cores. Furrr and doParallel seems to be similar in time efficiency
